jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase perfectly integrated into character. Maximum economy and character specificity.

jokes.text: Ha-ha!
jokes.punchline: Nelson's signature bullying laugh
jokes.explanation: Another classic character-specific catchphrase, though less impactful than Homer's D'oh.

jokes.text: Can't you just write on your arm like I do?
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining different types of paper for school
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude alternative to proper school supplies
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Homer's ignorance and cheapness.

jokes.text: These days, everything's done on computers. And staplers. Computers and staplers.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why he doesn't need school supplies
jokes.punchline: The random addition of staplers as equally important to computers
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with the unexpected addition and emphasis on staplers.

jokes.text: 'S' is for shiksa. S-H-I- Mmm-
jokes.setup: Krusty's educational toy spelling out words
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate Yiddish term on a children's toy
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural satire perfectly fitting Krusty's character as an inappropriate children's entertainer.

jokes.text: I think there's a 'T' in there somewhere. Ah, look it up.
jokes.setup: Struggling to spell a Yiddish word
jokes.punchline: Giving up and telling kids to look it up themselves
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's laziness and irresponsibility.

jokes.text: Don't you hate that we have to go back to stupid school tomorrow? - I like school. - Me too! We have so much in common.
jokes.setup: Alex trying to bond with Lisa over hating school
jokes.punchline: Lisa's earnest response completely missing the social cue, then fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Lisa's academic nature clashing with social expectations.

jokes.text: You have a pen glued to your cowlick. If you don't like it, it's gone! [Strains, Groans] - Uh, you want this? - No.
jokes.setup: Alex pointing out another student's pen stuck in hair
jokes.punchline: Student painfully removing it and awkward offer to give it away
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy with some cringe elements, but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Yo, Apu, give me the usual. Yes, sir. One Kwik-E dog, one bubble gum cigar... and the latest issue of Success magazine.
jokes.setup: Homer ordering his 'usual' from convenience store
jokes.punchline: The ironic inclusion of Success magazine with junk food
jokes.explanation: Good character-based irony showing Homer's delusions about bettering himself.

jokes.text: Hey, this hot dog tastes different. Yes. I just cleaned out the machine, sir. So the snack you are enjoying has not been soaking in putrid grease.
jokes.setup: Homer noticing hot dog tastes different
jokes.punchline: Revealing the normal state is 'soaking in putrid grease'
jokes.explanation: Well-structured joke revealing the disgusting reality of convenience store food.

jokes.text: Well, yeah. But without the grease... all you can taste is the hog anus.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about clean hot dog
jokes.punchline: Revealing what hot dogs are actually made of
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from the grease joke, with Homer's characteristic crudeness.

jokes.text: I'm so sorry, but I sold it all to the rendering plant. - People buy grease? - Oh, yes. They use it to make products such as soap, cosmetics, baby food.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining where the grease went
jokes.punchline: The horrifying list ending with baby food
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list with dark humor about industrial food production.

jokes.text: Used grease is worth money? [Gasps] Then my arteries are clogged with yellow gold! I'm rich, Apu! Rich and- [Groaning]
jokes.punchline: Connecting his clogged arteries to wealth, then immediately having heart problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor with perfect immediate consequence. Great metaphor and timing.

jokes.text: Uh, Dad, I don't think the dog can handle any more bacon. [Whimpering] Looks like he's about ready for another squeezin'
jokes.setup: Homer forcing bacon on the family dog
jokes.punchline: The dog's distress and Homer's obliviousness to animal cruelty for grease
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's obsession, though the animal cruelty element makes it less enjoyable.

jokes.text: Marge, if you don't mind, I'm a little busy right now... achieving financial independence. - With cans of grease? - No! Through savings and wise investments. Of course with grease!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to achieve financial independence
jokes.punchline: The fake-out denial followed by admission it's all about grease
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke showing Homer's grandiose delusions about his grease scheme.

jokes.text: Where do you think you're going? - It's the first day of school. - Not for you it isn't. You're in the grease business now.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to go to school
jokes.punchline: Homer declaring Bart is now in the grease business instead
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's priorities and poor parenting.

jokes.text: Then the doctor told me that both my eyes were lazy. And that's why it was the best summer ever.
jokes.punchline: Finding out about lazy eyes being the highlight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum logic - finding joy in something most would consider bad news.

jokes.text: Hey, blindy, have a nice trip.! [Ralph Screams] [Thud] Ha-ha.!
jokes.punchline: Ralph falling and Nelson's signature laugh
jokes.explanation: Standard bullying joke, elevated slightly by character specificity but still pretty basic.

jokes.text: I need a volunteer for a thankless chore. Shall I assume the only hand in the air is Lisa Simpson's? Thank you, Lisa.
jokes.punchline: Knowing only Lisa will volunteer for thankless work
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Lisa's eagerness and Skinner's exploitation of it.

jokes.text: But when you get to the trophy case, give her some cock-and-bull story. They're out for cleaning or whatever.
jokes.setup: Skinner giving Lisa instructions for showing new student around
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting the trophy case is empty and to lie about it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's casual dishonesty and the school's lack of achievements.

jokes.text: Your name's Lisa? Shut up! I love that name. Did she just tell me to shut up? Take it outside.
jokes.setup: Alex enthusiastically responding to Lisa's name
jokes.punchline: Lisa taking 'shut up' literally and being offended
jokes.explanation: Decent misunderstanding joke showing generational/cultural differences in language.

jokes.text: Is that perfume? - Oh! Don't be such a Phoebe. It's Pretension by Calvin Klein.
jokes.punchline: Made-up perfume name 'Pretension' and calling someone a 'Phoebe'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with the perfume name satirizing pretentious brands.

jokes.text: Well, you'll definitely wanna get yourself a good doll. The new Malibu Stacy has an achievable chest.
jokes.punchline: The bizarre selling point of a doll having an 'achievable' chest size
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of beauty standards and toy marketing, with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Oh.Jacks. Jacks are big. They went out for a while, but then they came roaring back.
jokes.punchline: Talking about jacks like they're a fashion trend that made a comeback
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Lisa's innocent view of playground trends as serious cultural movements.

jokes.text: Uh, they were all wiped out in the big trophy fire.
jokes.setup: Alex asking about empty trophy case
jokes.punchline: Lisa's improvised lie about a trophy fire instead of Skinner's suggested cleaning story
jokes.explanation: Good callback joke showing Lisa's honesty making her a terrible liar.

jokes.text: Ah! I see the trophies are still out for cleaning, eh, Lisa? [Chuckling] [Forced Chuckle]
jokes.setup: Skinner arriving and hearing Lisa's lie
jokes.punchline: Skinner's awkward attempt to correct the story and Lisa's uncomfortable response
jokes.explanation: Good callback creating awkward situation between Lisa and Skinner's conflicting lies.

jokes.text: Four pounds of grease. That comes to... 63 cents. - Whoo-hoo! - Dad, all that bacon cost $27.
jokes.punchline: Revealing he lost money on the deal
jokes.explanation: Well-structured joke revealing Homer's terrible business sense and mathematical skills.

jokes.text: Yeah, but your mom paid for that. But doesn't she get her money from you? And I get my money from grease. What's the problem?
jokes.setup: Bart pointing out the financial loss
jokes.punchline: Homer's circular logic justifying the loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely circular and nonsensical but delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: Um, I don't know how much more school I can miss. Oh, you'll miss plenty. I have a feeling this business... is going to consume our whole lives.
jokes.punchline: Homer's ominous prediction delivered enthusiastically
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Homer obliviously predicting disaster as a positive thing.

jokes.text: Ooh, twins. Which one is the evil one? [Laughing] [Forced Laughter]
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate question and the awkward response
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about twin stereotypes, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: [Cell Phone Ringing] Just a sec. Hello? Oh, yeah. Like I'd be seen with a Discover card. - You have a cell phone? - And a purse!
jokes.setup: Alex answering cell phone in school
jokes.punchline: The absurd status symbol hierarchy and other kids' amazement
jokes.explanation: Good period-specific humor about early cell phone status and credit card snobbery.

jokes.text: Maybe they went off to plan a surprise party for you. [Laughing] Oh, yeah. Good one, Mom.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to console Lisa about being ditched
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sarcastic response to the obviously false optimism
jokes.explanation: Decent family dynamic humor with Lisa's justified cynicism about Marge's naive optimism.

jokes.text: Oh, and get this, Mom. She drinks iced tea.
jokes.punchline: Treating iced tea as scandalously adult
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Lisa's innocence and what she considers shocking.

jokes.text: But, Dad- - Uh-uh. Think. Is that what Alex would say?
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately applying his terrible advice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's bad advice, showing he's already committed to this strategy.

jokes.text: Well, maybe you can put them on your doll. [Laughter]
jokes.punchline: Alex's condescending suggestion and others laughing
jokes.explanation: Basic mean girl humor, effective but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: You forgot apple bobbing. - No, I didn't.
jokes.setup: Student pointing out Lisa repeated apple bobbing
jokes.punchline: Lisa's defensive response to being caught in repetition
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing Lisa's embarrassment at being caught.

jokes.text: No, we weren't big on fruit. We were more into, like, dances. You know, things that are fun.
jokes.setup: Alex responding to apple pick description
jokes.punchline: Dismissing fruit-based activities as not fun
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the cultural clash between traditional and modern activities.

jokes.text: I don't know. The ponies might be startled by the loud music. - Well, there wouldn't be ponies. - Oh.
jokes.setup: Lisa expressing concern about dance vs apple pick
jokes.punchline: Her disappointed 'Oh' when learning no ponies
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Lisa's genuine disappointment about missing ponies.

jokes.text: School dance? I didn't approve any school dance. Yes, you did. Yesterday, right by my locker. - Oh, yeah. Yes. Um, carry on.
jokes.punchline: Immediately backing down when challenged by student
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Skinner's weakness and fear of confrontation.

jokes.text: There must be $20 worth of grease on his forehead alone.
jokes.punchline: Calculating the monetary value of a kid's acne
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor showing Homer's obsession making him see grease everywhere.

jokes.text: Can I help you, sir? My God, you're greasy! Mr. Maruko, help!
jokes.punchline: Being terrified by Homer's grease-obsessed appearance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how far Homer's grease obsession has gone.

jokes.text: If she didn't want her car ruined, she should have done a better job hiding her keys.
jokes.setup: Bart pointing out mom will be angry
jokes.punchline: Homer blaming Marge for not hiding keys better
jokes.explanation: Good character logic showing Homer's complete lack of personal responsibility.

jokes.text: Hey! What the- Hey.! Hey.! You're taking our grease.! - It's our grease now. - We run the grease racket in this town.
jokes.punchline: Organized crime controlling the grease trade
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of the grease plot into organized crime territory.

jokes.text: Hey, that's my shovel! We also run the shovel racket.
jokes.punchline: The criminals expanding their monopoly to shovels too
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and economy - the absurdity of organized crime controlling mundane tools.

jokes.text: Just, you know, add some accessories, lip gloss, maybe drop five pounds.
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting weight loss to a child
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about toxic beauty culture affecting children, delivered casually.

jokes.text: D.M.Y. - What's D.M.Y.? - 'Don't mess yourself.' - Ew! Yeah. That's why we changed it to D.M.Y.
jokes.punchline: The euphemistic acronym for something gross, changed for being gross
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay joke about euphemistic language and circular logic.

jokes.text: Am I the only one who just wants to play hopscotch... and bake cookies and watch The McLaughlin Group?
jokes.setup: Lisa frustrated with growing up pressure
jokes.punchline: The incongruous list ending with political talk show
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment perfectly capturing Lisa's unique blend of childishness and intellectual sophistication.

jokes.text: You could raise some emus. Emus? Really? Oh, that's pretty crazy. Nah.
jokes.punchline: Homer's brief consideration and dismissal of emu farming
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with the random emu farming suggestion.

jokes.text: Emu farm? [Laughing] You're priceless, Marge.
jokes.punchline: Treating it as ridiculous while his grease scheme is worse
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer's complete lack of self-awareness about his own schemes.

jokes.text: The thing about huckleberries is... once you've had fresh, you'll never go back to canned. Uh- Uh- So anyway, I kicked the guy's ass.
jokes.punchline: Other student's crude attempt to sound tough
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between intellectual and crude conversation styles at lunch.

jokes.text: No doubt. Can't a girl fabulize herself before the big dance?
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to act like Alex
jokes.punchline: Using made-up sophisticated vocabulary awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's awkward attempt at sophistication.

jokes.text: You've always had a crush on me, right? Well, this is your lucky day... 'cause you're gonna take me to the dance. Pretty great, huh? See ya.
jokes.setup: Lisa approaching Milhouse for a date
jokes.punchline: Her completely unromantic, transactional approach
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Lisa's desperation making her act unlike herself.

jokes.text: What am I doing? This isn't me. I'm sorry, Milhouse. I'm free next weekend! There's plenty of Milhouse to go around!
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's desperate response to any romantic possibility
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Lisa's self-awareness and Milhouse's pathetic eagerness.

jokes.text: Dear Lord, I know you're busy... seeing as how you can watch women changing clothes and all that. But if you help us steal this grease tonight... I promise we'll donate half the profits to charity. Dad, he's not stupid. All right, screw it. Let's roll!
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate prayer, failed negotiation, and giving up on God
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy with Homer's inappropriate understanding of God and casual abandonment of faith.

jokes.text: Dad, he's not stupid. All right, screw it. Let's roll!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to bargain with God
jokes.punchline: Immediate abandonment of religious approach
jokes.explanation: Homer's quick pivot from faith to crime. Bart as voice of reason creates good irony.

jokes.text: Lisa, I made you some homemade Pepsi for the dance. It's a little thick, but the price is right.
jokes.punchline: The concept of homemade Pepsi and its poor quality
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Marge's well-intentioned but misguided helpfulness.

jokes.text: Oh, honey. A baby couldn't have organized a big school dance... unless it was especially skilled. Or one of those super-babies from Brazil.
jokes.setup: Marge comforting Lisa about feeling like a baby
jokes.punchline: The random escalation to Brazilian super-babies
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with the unexpected Brazilian super-babies reference.

jokes.text: And if you sit there with a brave little smile and a festive bow... why, you could be the belle of the ball. Mom, you can't possibly believe that.
jokes.punchline: Lisa calling out the obvious delusion
jokes.explanation: Decent family dynamic with Lisa's realistic assessment of Marge's false optimism.

jokes.text: Uh, we're new foreign exchange students... from, uh, uh, Scotland! Saints be praised! I'm from Scotland. Where do you hail from? Uh, North Kilt Town.
jokes.punchline: Making up obviously fake Scottish town name
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with the obviously fake Scottish place name.

jokes.text: No fooling? I'm from North Kilttown. Do you know Angus McCloud? Wait a minute. There's no Angus McCloud in North Kilttown! Why, you're not from Scotland at all!
jokes.punchline: Catching Homer in the lie through local knowledge
jokes.explanation: Well-structured trap joke showing Willie's character knowledge catching Homer's deception.

jokes.text: Stop pummeling me! It's really painful! All right, fine. I'll strangle ya for a while!
jokes.punchline: Willie's accommodation by switching to strangling
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with Willie's 'helpful' accommodation of Homer's complaint.

jokes.text: Mother has a june bug cornered in the basement, and she needs me to finish it off.
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining why he has to leave
jokes.punchline: The bizarre domestic emergency with his mother
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment perfectly capturing Skinner's bizarre relationship with his domineering mother.

jokes.text: Oh, Lisa, it's terrible. Okay. This dance has gone Titanic.
jokes.punchline: Using 'Titanic' as an adjective for disaster
jokes.explanation: Decent period-specific reference using Titanic as disaster metaphor.

jokes.text: Come on, Alex. We've only got nine, maybe 1 0 years tops... where we can giggle in church and chew with our mouths open... and go days without bathing. We'll never have that freedom again.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to convince Alex to enjoy being a kid
jokes.punchline: The gross but accurate description of childhood freedoms
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with Lisa's wisdom about childhood being both gross and precious.

jokes.text: Huh? Look, it's snowing! Ah! The snowflake tastes like fish sticks.
jokes.punchline: Realizing it's actually grease that tastes like food
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag with the grease explosion creating fake snow with disgusting taste.

jokes.text: It's like a hamburger milk shake! Here comes a grease ball! [Grunts]
jokes.punchline: The disgusting but accurate description of grease texture
jokes.explanation: Decent gross-out humor with the unappetizing food comparison.

jokes.text: Hey, Luigi bring you kids free pizza. Why you have to make the fun? Huh?
jokes.setup: Luigi offering free pizza at the ruined dance
jokes.punchline: His confusion about why kids are playing in grease instead of eating
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Luigi's immigrant English and confusion about the situation.

jokes.text: You're playing in grease? Yarg! Oh, act your age. [Grunts, Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Willie disgusted by kids playing in grease
jokes.punchline: Student telling him to act his age while playing in grease
jokes.explanation: Good irony with child telling adult to act his age while doing something childish.
